verses.commentary: The third visit invokes Deuteronomy 19:15: every matter established by two or three witnesses. Paul positions the visit within biblical legal protocol for validation.
The warning already given on the second visit is now repeated while absent. On return, Paul will not spare those who sinned earlier or others. Discipline is promised, not threatened.
They demand proof that Christ speaks through Paul. The proof will come through Christ's power among them, not weakness. Authority will manifest unmistakably.

Christ was crucified in weakness yet lives by God's power. Paul is weak in Him yet will live with Him by God's power in dealing with them. Weakness and power paradoxically coexist in Christological pattern.
Examine and test yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Do you not realize Christ Jesus is in you, unless you fail the test? The examination shifts from Paul's authenticity to theirs.
Paul trusts they will discover he has not failed the test. His legitimacy will become evident through their own self-examination.
Prayer that they do nothing wrong, not to prove Paul has passed the test but so they will do right even if Paul seems to have failed. Their righteousness matters more than his vindication.
We cannot do anything against the truth, only for the truth. Truth constrains action absolutely; no power exists to oppose it, only to serve it.
Glad when weak but they are strong. Prayer is for their full restoration. Paul's weakness combined with their strength is the desired outcome, inverting normal honor seeking.
Writing while absent to avoid being harsh when present in using authority. Authority is for building up, not tearing down. Preventative correction preserves relationship.
Rejoice, strive for restoration, encourage one another, be of one mind, live in peace. The God of love and peace will be with you. The imperative list culminates in promise of divine presence.
Greet one another with a holy kiss. Physical expression of reconciliation and affection is commanded, making abstract unity tangible.
All God's people send greetings. The broader church extends fellowship, connecting the Corinthians to the universal body.

The Trinitarian benediction: grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, love of God, fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all. Each person of the Trinity contributes distinct blessing, yet the benediction is unified.
